understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of KA59 is to complete each level by moving all selectable pieces onto goal tiles (marked '+') simultaneously. The final level must be solved with all goals covered to trigger a WIN, which ends the game.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: A level consists of a grid with walls (obstacles), one or more movable pieces, and one or more goal tiles. The player clicks to select a piece and then clicks on an empty tile to move the selected piece to that destination. Movement appears to be in a straight line along clear paths (likely cardinal directions only), and the piece travels instantly to the clicked location if unobstructed; if blocked by a wall or another piece, the piece does not move. Only one piece is selected and moved per action. Each level has a limited step budget; running out of steps without covering all goals results in failure. There is no explicit 'push' mechanic observed; pieces do not seem to move other pieces. The win condition requires every '+' goal tile to be occupied by any piece at the same time.
